display_name: ARP suppression in EVPN means you won't see ARP on the wire — absence is not a problem
context: Troubleshooting host reachability in a VXLAN/EVPN fabric where ARP suppression (proxy ARP/ND) is enabled on the layer-2 VNI
observation: ARP requests from a host are not seen flooding across the fabric, or ARP counters on remote leaves stay flat while hosts still communicate
naive_interpretation: ARP is broken, the host is not ARPing, or BUM flooding is failing somewhere in the fabric
correct_interpretation: With ARP suppression enabled, the ingress leaf answers ARP requests locally from the EVPN control plane (the MAC/IP advertised in Type-2 routes) instead of flooding the request across the fabric. The absence of fabric-wide ARP flooding is the feature working as designed, not a fault. ARP only floods when the leaf has no cached binding for the requested IP and falls back to flood-and-learn.
why: EVPN advertises host MAC+IP bindings as Type-2 (MAC/IP Advertisement) routes; each leaf builds a local ARP/ND proxy table from these advertisements
RFC 9161 defines the proxy ARP/ND behavior: the leaf intercepts the ARP request and replies on behalf of the remote host when it has a binding, suppressing the broadcast
Suppression dramatically reduces BUM (broadcast/unknown-unicast/multicast) traffic in large fabrics — flat ARP counters across the fabric are the expected steady state
A genuine ARP problem shows up as missing Type-2 routes for the destination IP, not as missing ARP floods

confirmation_commands.purpose: Confirm ARP suppression is active and list the locally cached host bindings the leaf is answering from
Verify Type-2 MAC/IP routes for the destination host exist in the EVPN table
Confirm proxy ARP entries the leaf uses to answer locally

traps: Concluding ARP or flooding is broken because ARP requests don't appear across the fabric — suppression is meant to stop exactly that
Packet-capturing on a remote leaf for ARP and finding nothing, then chasing a phantom flooding problem
Forgetting that suppression only works when the EVPN Type-2 route exists — the real check is whether the binding was advertised, not whether ARP flooded
Disabling ARP suppression to 'fix' a problem that was actually a missing Type-2 route, masking the real control-plane issue

sources.title: RFC 9161 — Operational Aspects of Proxy ARP/ND in EVPN Networks
